sources.title: Turmeric-associated liver injury: a systematic review
Liver Injury Associated with Turmeric—a Growing Problem: Ten Cases from DILIN
The influence of piperine on the pharmacokinetics of fexofenadine
Bacopa monnieri as a Natural Nootropic: Narrative Review of Current Evidence
The Safety of Berberine for the Treatment of Metabolic Disorders: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
Berberine: a review of its pharmacokinetics properties and therapeutic potentials
Repeated administration of berberine inhibits cytochromes P450 in humans
The effects of berberine on the pharmacokinetics of cyclosporin A in healthy volunteers
A systematic review of the potential adverse effects of caffeine consumption in healthy adults, pregnant women, adolescents, and children
Safety of Supplementation with Omega-3 Fatty Acids in Randomized Clinical Trials: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis
Effect of Long-Term Marine Omega-3 Fatty Acids Supplementation on the Risk of Atrial Fibrillation
Ashwagandha-induced liver injury—A case series from India and literature review
Safety of Ashwagandha During Pregnancy: A Systematic Review

changes.new_value: Severe renal impairment or kidney failure without clinician supervision because reduced magnesium clearance increases hypermagnesemia risk.
changes.rationale: Replaces a token with supported, readable renal guidance.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: magnesium supplements can cause dose-related gastrointestinal effects. Severe renal impairment increases hypermagnesemia risk, and magnesium can reduce absorption of some oral antibiotics and bisphosphonates; pregnancy, breastfeeding, and long-term product-specific evidence remain limited.
changes.rationale: Adds an evidence-labelled, formulation-bounded summary.
Removes a severity token because mixture-level evidence cannot define an isolated-compound contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Human evidence is mostly formulation-dependent. High-dose curcuminoids may cause GI upset and interact with anticoagulants/antiplatelets.
changes.new_value: Human safety evidence is formulation-dependent and concerns curcuminoid mixtures or turmeric products, not isolated bisdemethoxycurcumin.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported constituent-level interaction claim.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: no adequate isolated-compound human safety profile. Reports involving turmeric or mixed curcuminoids cannot establish bisdemethoxycurcumin-specific adverse effects, contraindications, interactions, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, liver, or kidney guidance.
changes.rationale: States the exact constituent evidence boundary.
Removes a severity token because mixture-level evidence cannot define an isolated-compound contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Human evidence is mostly formulation-dependent. High-dose curcuminoids may cause GI upset and interact with anticoagulants/antiplatelets.
changes.new_value: Human safety evidence is formulation-dependent and concerns curcuminoid mixtures or turmeric products, not isolated demethoxycurcumin.
changes.rationale: Removes an unsupported constituent-level interaction claim.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: no adequate isolated-compound human safety profile. Reports involving turmeric or mixed curcuminoids cannot establish demethoxycurcumin-specific adverse effects, contraindications, interactions, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, liver, or kidney guidance.
changes.rationale: States the exact constituent evidence boundary.
Whole-extract trials cannot establish an isolated-constituent pregnancy contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: May cause GI irritation or hemolysis risk in isolated/high-dose contexts; herb-specific safety and pregnancy cautions apply.
changes.new_value: Human safety evidence concerns whole Bacopa extracts, not isolated bacopaside II; constituent-specific adverse effects and contraindications remain unknown.
changes.rationale: Removes unsupported isolated-compound hemolysis and pregnancy guidance.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: no adequate isolated-bacopaside II human safety profile. Gastrointestinal findings from whole Bacopa extracts cannot define isolated-compound dose, incidence, interactions, pregnancy, breastfeeding, liver, kidney, or surgery guidance.
changes.rationale: Prevents whole-extract evidence from being assigned to one constituent.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: turmeric-associated liver injury is uncommon but documented, including recurrence after re-exposure. Piperine altered exposure to a P-glycoprotein substrate in a small human study; product bioavailability and medication context matter, while pregnancy and breastfeeding evidence remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Combines formulation-matched liver and pharmacokinetic evidence without class-wide extrapolation.

changes.expected_old_value: Interacts with CYP enzymes; caution with antidiabetics.
changes.new_value: Pregnancy, breastfeeding, and use in infants because berberine can displace bilirubin and poses kernicterus risk.
changes.rationale: Separates contraindications from medication interactions.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: gastrointestinal adverse effects are most commonly reported. Avoid during pregnancy, breastfeeding, and in infants; repeated human dosing inhibited CYP2D6, CYP2C9, and CYP3A4, and a clinical cyclosporine interaction is documented, so medication review is important.
changes.rationale: Adds evidence-labelled reproductive, adverse-effect, and interaction guidance.

changes.new_value: Pregnancy, breastfeeding, and use in infants because berberine can displace bilirubin and poses kernicterus risk.
changes.rationale: Replaces a token with complete supported guidance.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: evidence is primarily for berberine rather than hydrochloride-form comparisons. Gastrointestinal effects are common; avoid during pregnancy, breastfeeding, and in infants, and review cyclosporine or medicines dependent on CYP2D6, CYP2C9, or CYP3A4.
changes.rationale: Publishes shared-moiety evidence while noting the form-specific limitation.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: adverse effects are dose- and susceptibility-dependent and include sleep disruption, anxiety-like symptoms, and cardiovascular effects. Pregnancy guidance uses lower intake thresholds; total intake from all sources and concurrent stimulants should be reviewed.
changes.rationale: Adds a concise evidence-labelled dose-context summary.

changes.expected_old_value: Kidney disease or renal impairment due to hypermagnesemia risk; myasthenia gravis may worsen with high-dose magnesium; bowel obstruction risk for high-dose laxative-effect magnesium salts such as citrate or oxide; May reduce absorption of tetracycline or fluoroquinolone antibiotics unless doses are separated by several hours; may reduce absorption of bisphosphonate osteoporosis medication; can add to the effect of muscle relaxants or other CNS depressants; loop or thiazide diuretics can alter magnesium levels in either direction.
changes.new_value: Severe renal impairment or kidney failure without clinician supervision because reduced magnesium clearance increases hypermagnesemia risk.
changes.rationale: Retains the supported contraindication and moves documented interactions to their structured field.

changes.expected_old_value: May support sleep quality and mild anxiety/stress in selected adults; evidence is promising but heterogeneous. | Do not claim it treats insomnia or anxiety disorders; do not imply all forms are equivalent. | Create/upgrade as supportive sleep/anxiety page with form-specific dosing caveat.
changes.new_value: Safety evidence: supplemental magnesium can cause dose-related gastrointestinal effects. Severe renal impairment increases hypermagnesemia risk, and magnesium can reduce absorption of some oral antibiotics and bisphosphonates; safety and tolerability vary by salt and dose.
changes.rationale: Replaces promotional efficacy copy with structured safety guidance.
Removes a severity token because reviews do not establish a categorical contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: GI upset; bleeding risk at high doses.
changes.new_value: Mild gastrointestinal effects may occur. Higher-dose, long-term marine omega-3 trials show an atrial-fibrillation signal; clinically significant bleeding is not established at common studied doses.
changes.rationale: Replaces a categorical bleeding claim with the systematic-review evidence.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: mild gastrointestinal adverse effects occur, while clinically significant bleeding is not established at common studied doses. Higher-dose, long-term marine omega-3 trials show an atrial-fibrillation signal; anticoagulant use warrants monitoring and medication review.
changes.rationale: Adds balanced bleeding and atrial-fibrillation evidence.
Removes a severity token because trials do not establish a categorical contraindication.

changes.expected_old_value: Often causes GI side effects; cognitive effects require weeks, not acute use.
changes.new_value: Short-term trials of Bacopa extracts most often report gastrointestinal adverse effects; preparations and safety reporting vary.
changes.rationale: Keeps safety content separate from efficacy timing.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: short-term human trials of Bacopa extracts most often report gastrointestinal adverse effects, but preparations and reporting vary. Pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, long-term use, organ impairment, and clinically significant medication interactions remain insufficiently characterized.
changes.rationale: Adds bounded whole-extract tolerability and explicit evidence gaps.
Removes a token because combination-specific categorical contraindications are not established.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: the combination's risk is strongly influenced by caffeine dose and individual sensitivity. Sleep disruption, anxiety-like symptoms, cardiovascular effects, pregnancy intake limits, total caffeine exposure, and concurrent stimulants require attention; long-term combination-specific evidence remains limited.
changes.rationale: Adds component-grounded guidance while preserving the combination evidence limit.

changes.new_value: Previous turmeric- or curcumin-associated liver injury because recurrence after re-exposure has been reported.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token with a supported disease-specific flag.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: turmeric-associated liver injury is uncommon but documented, including recurrence after re-exposure. Because phytosome products are designed to increase exposure, formulation-specific safety matters; pregnancy, breastfeeding, bleeding, surgery, and medication-interaction evidence for this form remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Adds the documented liver signal without inferring unproven interactions.

changes.new_value: Pregnancy; previous ashwagandha-associated liver injury; existing chronic liver disease warrants clinician review because severe cases have been reported.
changes.rationale: Replaces tokens with evidence-qualified reproductive and liver flags.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: short-term tolerability evidence is product-specific, while rare clinically significant liver injury is documented and can be severe with pre-existing chronic liver disease. Avoid during pregnancy; breastfeeding, surgery, thyroid, autoimmune, and medication-interaction evidence remain incomplete.
changes.rationale: Adds evidence-labelled liver and pregnancy guidance with explicit remaining uncertainty.

changes.new_value: Pregnancy; previous ashwagandha-associated liver injury; existing chronic liver disease warrants clinician review because severe cases have been reported.
changes.rationale: Replaces a severity token with evidence-qualified reproductive and liver flags.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: short-term tolerability evidence varies by extract, while rare clinically significant liver injury is documented and can be severe with pre-existing chronic liver disease. Avoid during pregnancy; breastfeeding, surgery, thyroid, autoimmune, and medication-interaction evidence remain incomplete.
changes.rationale: Adds evidence-labelled liver and pregnancy guidance with formulation limits.

changes.new_value: Severe renal impairment or kidney failure without clinician supervision because reduced magnesium clearance increases hypermagnesemia risk; other risks depend on the full electrolyte formula.
changes.rationale: Replaces a token and preserves the multi-ingredient limitation.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: magnesium can cause dose-related gastrointestinal effects and severe renal impairment increases hypermagnesemia risk. It can reduce absorption of some oral antibiotics and bisphosphonates; all other risks depend on the blend's exact electrolytes and doses.
changes.rationale: Adds supported magnesium guidance while preserving formula uncertainty.

changes.expected_old_value: Hypotension or antihypertensive medication use given a mild blood-pressure-lowering effect; May add to sedation with benzodiazepines or alcohol or other CNS depressants; limited safety data in pregnancy or breastfeeding.
changes.rationale: Removes interaction claims not established by the human systematic review.

changes.expected_old_value: Generally well tolerated; caution if prone to low blood pressure or stacking multiple calming agents.
changes.new_value: Short-term trials reported no serious adverse events, but long-term, pregnancy, breastfeeding, and medication-interaction safety remain insufficient.
changes.rationale: Replaces inferred blood-pressure and sedation cautions with reviewed evidence limits.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: a systematic review of short-term trials reported no serious adverse events, but study populations and dosing varied. Long-term use, pregnancy, breastfeeding, surgery, organ impairment, and clinically significant medication interactions remain insufficiently characterized.
changes.rationale: Adds bounded short-term tolerability without inferred interactions.

changes.new_value: Severe renal impairment or kidney failure without clinician supervision because reduced magnesium clearance increases hypermagnesemia risk.
changes.rationale: Clarifies the supported renal risk.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: magnesium supplements can cause dose-related gastrointestinal effects. Severe renal impairment increases hypermagnesemia risk, and magnesium can reduce absorption of some oral antibiotics and bisphosphonates; glycinate-specific comparative safety evidence remains limited.
changes.rationale: Adds evidence-labelled guidance and a form-specific evidence limit.
Moves a precaution to interactions because no categorical contraindication is established.

changes.new_value: Mild gastrointestinal effects may occur. Higher-dose, long-term marine omega-3 trials show an atrial-fibrillation signal; clinically significant bleeding is not established at common studied doses.
changes.rationale: Replaces a blanket statement with clinically significant reviewed findings.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: mild gastrointestinal adverse effects occur, while clinically significant bleeding is not established at common studied doses. Higher-dose, long-term marine omega-3 trials show an atrial-fibrillation signal; anticoagulant use warrants monitoring and medication review.
changes.rationale: Adds balanced bleeding and atrial-fibrillation evidence.
Moves a precaution to interactions because no categorical contraindication is established.

changes.new_value: Mild gastrointestinal effects may occur. Higher-dose, long-term marine omega-3 trials show an atrial-fibrillation signal; clinically significant bleeding is not established at common studied doses.
changes.rationale: Adds dose context and the bleeding evidence balance.

changes.new_value: Safety evidence: mild gastrointestinal adverse effects occur, while clinically significant bleeding is not established at common studied doses. Higher-dose, long-term marine omega-3 trials show an atrial-fibrillation signal; anticoagulant use warrants monitoring and medication review.
changes.rationale: Adds balanced bleeding and atrial-fibrillation evidence.
